FIRE AND SAN DIEGO CITY LIFEGUARD SWIFT RESCUE REPORTED AT LEAST 3 VEHICLES UNDER WATER AND RESPONDED TO HIGH WATER RESCUES. HOMES ON TORRANCE AND REYNARD WERE THREATENED.
SEVERAL HOMES FLOODED. SOME RESIDENT EVACUATIONS UNDERWAY. GARAGE AND MINOR HOME DAMAGE REPORTED.

A RESIDENT AND THEIR CHILD ON LAWRENCE LANE WERE STRANDED IN THEIR HOME BY FLOOD WATERS.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED MUD FLOWING IN SILVERADO CANYON, BLOCKING BOTH LANES OF SMISEK RANCH AND SILVERADO CANYON ROADS.

FLOODING STARTED AROUND 6:45 PM AND CONTINUED FOR A COUPLE HOURS. WIDE SPREAD FLASH FLOOD/ROAD CLOSURES ALONG ROUTE 2 IN THE WRIGHTWOOD AREA, DUE TO HEAVY RAINFALL IN THE AREA AND DEBRIS BEING WASHED ONTO ROADS. A COUPLE OF ROAD WASH OUTS AND CULVERTS BEING DAMAGED. THERE WERE A COUPLE OF SWIFT WATER RESCUES AS PEOPLE DROVE INTO FLOODED ROADWAYS. SOME MINOR STREET FLOODS IN THE HESPERIA, CA AREA AS WELL. FLOODING AND A SWIFT WATER RESCUES IN LYTLE CREEK, JUST BELOW BONITA FALLS. OCCURRED JUST BEFORE 8 PM. NO INJURIES. OBSERVER IS FROM HESPERIA WORKING IN ASSOCIATION WITH SAN BERNARDINO EMERGENCY SERVICE. OBSERVER LOCATION HESPERIA.
SWIFT WATER RESCUE AT LYTLE CREEK DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.

HIGHWAY 18 FLOODED WITH PORTIONS OF ROADWAY WASHING AWAY. SEVERAL VEHICLES STUCK IN MUD.

MULTIPLE LARGE TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN IN PALM DESERT COUNTRY CLUB AREA. TREES FELL ON APARTMENT ON ELKHORN TRAIL AND DAMAGED ROOF. 5288 CUSTOMERS LOST POWER DUE TO DOWNED LINES.
FRED WARING DR BETWEEN EL DORADO AND WARNER TRAIL IN PALM DESERT CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING AND DEBRIS.

SURVEY OF DAMAGE AREA BY NWS SHOWS NEAR 2 MILES OF STEEL POWER POLES DAMAGED. 1 MILE OF WOODEN POLES DAMAGED. THIS IS ALONG HIGHWAY 86 BETWEEN AVENUE 64 AND 68. TIME RANGE OF DAMAGE IS ESTIMATED 745 TO 800 PM.

MULTIPLE INJURIES AT A MOBILE HOME PARK WHERE SEVERAL OUTDOOR WORKERS WERE TOSSED BY THE WIND. ELSEWHERE IN THE THERMAL AREA, 15 POWERPOLES DOWNED (EXACT LOCATION UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME), VEHICLE STRUCK BY FALLING POWERPOLE WITH MULTIPLE INJURIES (LOCATION UNKNOWN) AND SERIOUS INJURIES AT PHILMORE JUST NORTH OF AVENUE 70. FLASH FLOODING HIGHWAY S2 FROM MILE MARKER 36 TO MILE MARKER 44. SOUTHBOUND LANES ARE BLOCKED.

CHP REPORTS A ROCK OR MUDSLIDE BLOCKING THE ROADWAY AT HIGHWAY S-22 NEAR SAN FELIPE ROAD.

*** 1 FATAL *** FLASH FLOOD AT MILL CREEK CROSSING...40 FEET WIDE AND 8 TO 12 INCHES DEEP. THE HEAD OF THE FLASH FLOOD WAS 3 TO 4 FEET DEEP. FROM VICTOR VALLEY NEWS GROUP: FLOOD WATERS OVERTOOK A COUPLE HIKING. AN OFF-DUTY SHERIFF'S DEPUTY HEARD THE COUPLE CALLING FOR HELP. HE WAS ABLE TO SAVE THE YOUNG WOMAN, BUT THE MAN WAS SWEPT AWAY. THE BODY OF THE VICTIM, A RANCHO CUCAMONGA MAN IN HIS 20S, WAS FOUND SHORTLY AFTER 7 P.M.
FLASH FLOOD AT MILL CREEK CROSSING...40 FEET WIDE AND 8 TO 12 INCHES DEEP. THE HEAD OF THE FLASH FLOOD WAS 3 TO 4 FEET DEEP.

MULTIPLE SWIFT WATER RESCUES ALONG BEAR VALLEY ROAD. WATER FLOWING FAST DOWN ROAD...WITH SOME AREAS OF WATER AROUND 5 FEET DEEP.

FLASH FLOOD OF HIGHWAY 79 IN AGUANGA. 1 FOOT OF MUD ON THE ROADS. CREWS WERE STILL CLEANING UP MUD ON THURSDAY.
SEVERAL LARGE ROCKS (ABOUT 7 IN DIAMETER) AND MUD SCATTERED ACROSS ALL LANES OF HIGHWAY 74, CAUSING VEHICLES TO GET FLAT TIRES.
LARGE EUCALYPTUS TREES DOWNED, BLOCKING ROADWAYS. NUMEROUS PALM FRONDS IN ROADWAYS.
A MALE AND FEMALE WERE IN A FLOOD CONTROL CHANNEL AND HAD TO BE RESCUED BY THE ORANGE COUNTY FIRE DEPT AROUND 4:30 PM. THEY WERE CAUGHT OFF GUARD BY THE QUICKLY RISING WATER.
DEBRIS FLOW AT SILVERADO CANYON ROAD AND OAK LANE. ROAD STILL PASSABLE.
DEBRIS FLOW 2 FEET DEEP OVER SILVERADO CANYON ROAD AT MAPLE SPRINGS TRUCK TRAIL.
ONE INCH REPORTED AT 3:30 PM.
A LANDSPOUT TORNADO WAS OBSERVED NEAR MENIFEE AROUND 2:45 PM. VIDEO AND PHOTOS WERE PROVIDED BY THE VIEWER AND THE LOCAL CBS NEWS STATION. NO DAMAGE REPORTED.
NEWS MEDIA REPORTS THAT TWO BLOCKS WERE IMPASSABLE WITH WATER UP TO THE DOORS OF VEHICLES ON COLLEGE AVE OFF STATE ROUTE 94.
SUDDEN WIND GUST AT PALM SPRINGS. NEAR ZERO VISIBILITY FOR A FEW MINUTES DUE TO BLOWING DUST. WIND GUST AT PALM SPRINGS AIRPORT OF 54 MPH AT THIS TIME.
